violations.description: Demonstration of knowledge by person in charge. — --Person in charge did not demonstrate adequate knowledge of the food service code requirements. Person in charge stated that reheated items needed to be cooked to 160F when asked. She also did not know the proper time for reheating items. Potentially hazardous foods should be reheated to 165F with-in 1 hour.

Duties of person in charge. — --Person in charge did not ensure employee compliance with an aspect of the food service operation specified in the food service code. Cooking temperatures were not checked for items on roller grill and sausage gravy in hot hold unit. Temperatures should be checked on all heated items before serving to make sure they are properly heated. Person in charge should make sure that food temperatures are checked before they are ready for purchase.
Handwashing, good hygienic practices (5 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (1 pt deducted)
Bare hand contact avoided, single- use gloves, and utensils used (3 pts deducted)
Hot holding and cold holding temperatures maintained, unwrapped food not reserved (5 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (1 pt deducted)
Number, location, accessibility, soap and paper towels provided (3 pts deducted)
Clean, proper use, storage, sanitizing solution (2 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (1 pt deducted)
Properly designed, located, constructed, operated, maintained, cleaned and chemical test kit provided (2 pts deducted)
